scenario_results.reason: Required the missing user, device, timing, and attempted-step details before giving bounded frontline guidance.
Produced a useful first-line troubleshooting order and explicit collaboration-platform escalation trigger without widening scope.
Applied remote-session consent, role, and privacy constraints before recommending remote assistance.
Prioritized by user scope and operational impact instead of executive seniority.
Created a clean endpoint-team handoff with captured evidence and no direct policy or device changes.
Handled communication constraints conservatively without bypassing identity or security controls.
Separated break-fix support from standard fulfillment and routed the adjacent request lane correctly.
Escalated broad multi-site impact to incident management instead of treating it as isolated user support.
Required user confirmation and workaround-limit documentation before closure.
Rejected direct technical execution clearly while preserving the support context for downstream specialists.
Handled conflicting device evidence with explicit uncertainty and escalation rather than guesswork.
Stayed within the reviewed local baseline and returned packaging notes without relying on stale delegated output.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the issue #119 requirements, including normal support guidance, ticket and escalation quality, service-impact prioritization, edge handling, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification was recorded in the readiness evidence.
